description_long: This is a dark blue, twin-engine military airplane with a robust fuselage and a low-mounted, straight wing configuration. The aircraft features two propeller engines mounted on the wings, each with a three-blade propeller and yellow-tipped blades. The tail section is conventional, with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers extending from either side. Markings on the fuselage and wings include 'MARINES', 'WT', and a white star insignia, indicating U.S. military service. The cockpit is positioned at the front of the fuselage and appears to be enclosed, with a clear canopy. The landing gear is tricycle-type, with two main wheels under the wings and a nose wheel under the cockpit.
description: A dark blue, twin-propeller military airplane with low-mounted straight wings, a single vertical tail, and U.S. Marine markings. The cockpit is enclosed and positioned at the front, with tricycle landing gear visible.
description_view_0: Side view showing the twin engines, propellers, and detailed U.S. Marine markings along the fuselage and wing.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the straight wingspan, twin engines, and the single vertical tail fin with 'WT' marking.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the propeller engines, landing gear, and military insignia on the fuselage and wing.
description_view_3: Front view emphasizing the cockpit canopy, nose wheel, and the symmetrical placement of the engines and wings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, straight wings with no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin piston engines mounted on the wings, each with a three-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main wheels and a single nose wheel, all fixed and visible.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a clear canopy, likely multi-seat.
